question.text: A trader has total margin of Rs. 10,00,000 with current utilization at 85%. If the order placement requires additional Rs. 60,000 margin, what will be the new utilization percentage?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Current margin used = 85% of 10,00,000 = Rs. 8,50,000. New total = 8,50,000 + 60,000 = Rs. 9,10,000. New utilization = 9,10,000 / 10,00,000 = 91%. This would trigger the 90% auto-cancellation of pending orders. **The Trap:** 85% ignores the new order. 90% miscalculates. 88% underestimates. **Cross-Exam Context:** Margin utilization monitoring is tested in all exchange-traded derivatives exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Crossing 90% utilization triggers automatic cancellation of all pending orders. Monitor your margin before placing new orders.
